questions: I feel things deeply and care about what happens. My nephew just scored his first goal in a soccer game and ran straight to me in the stands — what do I say to him?
I feel things deeply and care about what happens. A close friend just told me they got into their dream graduate program after three years of applying — how do I respond?
I feel things deeply and care about what happens. My neighbor's house caught fire last night and they lost everything — what do I say when I see them?
I feel things deeply and care about what happens. My team just finished a difficult six-month project and the client loved it — what do I tell the team?
I feel things deeply and care about what happens. My younger sister just called to say she passed her medical board exams — how do I respond?
I feel things deeply and care about what happens. I just found out a coworker I liked is being laid off after fifteen years at the company — what do I say to them?
I feel things deeply and care about what happens. My grandmother just called to say she finished reading the book I recommended and loved it — what do I tell her?
I feel things deeply and care about what happens. A student I mentored just sent me a message saying they got their first job offer — how do I respond?
I feel things deeply and care about what happens. My city just announced it's cutting funding for the public library system — what do I think about that?
I feel things deeply and care about what happens. My old college roommate just reached out after five years to say he's thinking of me — what do I write back?
